title: WS2: Candidate deduplication logic
description:
# WS2: Candidate Deduplication on CV Upload

## Context
When a CV is uploaded, we need to determine if the candidate already exists to avoid duplicates.

## Logic Flow
1. Parse CV → extract email and name
2. If email exists in parsed data:
   a. Query candidates table by email (has unique index: uq_candidates_email)
   b. If found: return existing candidate for merge confirmation
3. If no email or no email match:
   a. Query candidates by name (ILIKE fuzzy match)
   b. If similar found: return candidates for user selection
4. If no match found: create new candidate

## What to Build

### New function in src/services/candidates.ts
findDuplicateCandidate(parsed: ParsedCV): Promise<{ exact?: Candidate, similar: Candidate[] }>

- Email match = exact (100% confidence)
- Name match = similar (needs user confirmation)
- Returns empty if no matches

### Merge Logic
When user confirms merge:
- Update existing candidate with new data from CV
- Preserve manually-entered data that CV doesn't override
- Update resumeRaw and resumeParsedAt
- Regenerate embedding with new data

## Dependencies
- CV parsing service (provides parsed data)
- Existing candidates service (src/services/candidates.ts)

## Testing
- findDuplicateCandidate function exported
- Source contains email lookup query
- Source contains name ILIKE query
close_reason: Wave 3 complete: structured matching, candidate dedup, and radar chart implemented with passing tests

title: WS3: Skills tag cloud component with proficiency bars
description:
# WS3: Skills Tag Cloud with Proficiency Bars

## New: components/skills-tags.tsx

Detailed skills display with colored tags and proficiency bars.

### Features
- Hard skills: blue/primary-tinted tags
- Soft skills: purple/violet-tinted tags
- Each tag shows: skill name + proficiency bar (1-5 filled dots or bar segments)
- Source indicator badge: "CV" (auto-extracted) vs "Handmatig" (manual entry)
- Sortable: by proficiency (highest first) or alphabetical
- Filterable: show all / hard only / soft only
- Responsive: wraps to multiple rows on narrow screens

### Props Interface
```typescript
interface SkillsTagsProps {
  skills: StructuredSkills;
  sortBy?: "proficiency" | "alphabetical";
  filter?: "all" | "hard" | "soft";
  className?: string;
}
```

### Proficiency Display
Use 5 small circles/dots:
- Filled = achieved level
- Empty = remaining
- Color matches skill type (blue for hard, purple for soft)

### Dutch Strings
- "Harde vaardigheden" — hard skills section header
- "Zachte vaardigheden" — soft skills section header
- "Sorteer op" — sort dropdown
- "Toon" — filter dropdown

## Dependencies
- Shared Zod schemas (structuredSkillsSchema)

## Testing
- Component exports SkillsTags
- Source contains proficiency rendering logic
close_reason: Wave 2 complete: requirement extraction, CV parser, and skills tags all implemented with passing tests

title: WS1: Match detail UI component (structured results display)
description:
# WS1: Match Detail UI — Structured Results Display

## What to Build

### New: app/matching/match-detail.tsx
Client component that displays structured match results in a rich, scannable format.

Sections:
1. **Header** — Overall score badge + recommendation (go/no-go/conditional) + confidence
2. **Knock-out Criteria** — Pass/fail badges with evidence. Red alert if ANY knockout fails. Uses CheckCircle2/XCircle icons.
3. **Gunningscriteria** — Star ratings (1-5) per criterion with evidence text. Use filled/empty star icons. Color: primary for high (4-5), yellow for medium (3), red for low (1-2).
4. **Process Requirements** — Listed but not scored. Muted text.
5. **Risk Profile** — Amber/red flag cards listing risk areas
6. **Enrichment Suggestions** — Numbered list of actionable suggestions for the recruiter
7. **Reasoning Summary** — 2-3 sentence AI-generated recommendation reasoning

### Modify: app/matching/page.tsx
Update match cards to:
- Show structured data when criteriaBreakdown is available
- Add expandable detail section or link to match detail view
- Keep existing simple view for legacy matches (model !== "marienne-v1")
- Add "Gestructureerde analyse" button to trigger structured matching for pending matches

## UI Patterns
- Use existing Card, Badge components from components/ui/
- Follow Dutch UI string convention (English code variables)
- Responsive: works on mobile and desktop
- Use semantic Tailwind theme tokens (not hardcoded colors)

## Dependencies
- Structured matching service and API route must exist
- Schema migration must be complete (criteriaBreakdown column)

title: WS2: CV upload API route (multipart/form-data)
description:
# WS2: CV Upload API Endpoint

## New: app/api/cv-upload/route.ts

POST endpoint accepting multipart/form-data with CV file.

### Flow
1. Parse multipart form data (use Next.js built-in or formidable)
2. Validate file: type (application/pdf, application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document), size (max 20MB)
3. Upload file to Vercel Blob via file-storage.ts
4. Call parseCV(buffer, mimeType) from cv-parser service
5. Call findDuplicateCandidate(parsed) for dedup check
6. Return { parsed: ParsedCV, fileUrl: string, duplicates: { exact?: Candidate, similar: Candidate[] } }

### Save Endpoint
POST app/api/cv-upload/save/route.ts (or same route with action param):
- Accepts { parsed: ParsedCV, fileUrl: string, existingCandidateId?: string }
- Creates new candidate or merges with existing
- Stores resumeRaw, resumeParsedAt, skillsStructured
- Generates embedding for new/updated candidate
- Returns created/updated candidate record

### Pattern
Use withApiHandler wrapper. Follow existing API route patterns.

## Dependencies
- File storage (Vercel Blob)
- CV parsing service
- Dedup logic

## Testing
- Route file exists and exports POST handler
- Source contains multipart parsing
- Source contains file type validation
close_reason: Wave 4 complete: match API, CV upload routes, skills profile section, report generator

title: WS3: Install recharts + radar chart component
description:
# WS3: Skills Radar Chart Component

## Setup
pnpm add recharts

## New: components/skills-radar.tsx

Radar/spider chart using recharts RadarChart component. Shows candidate skills at a glance.

### Features
- Groups skills into categories: Technical, Soft Skills, Domain, Tools, Languages
- Each axis = one skill category, value = average proficiency (1-5) of skills in that category
- Minimum 3 axes required; falls back to horizontal BarChart below 3 categories
- Supports comparison mode: overlay two candidates (different colors) on same chart
- Responsive: auto-sizes to container
- Theme-aware: uses Tailwind theme tokens for colors

### Props Interface
```typescript
interface SkillsRadarProps {
  skills: StructuredSkills; // from candidate-intelligence schema
  compareWith?: StructuredSkills; // optional second candidate for overlay
  className?: string;
}
```

### Category Mapping
The component maps individual skills to categories using a simple keyword-based classifier:
- Technical: programming languages, frameworks, databases
- Domain: industry-specific (finance, healthcare, government)
- Tools: software tools (Jira, Excel, SAP)
- Soft Skills: communication, leadership, teamwork
- Languages: spoken languages

### Color Scheme
- Primary candidate: theme primary color
- Comparison candidate: theme secondary/accent color
- Grid: border color at low opacity

## Dependencies
- Shared Zod schemas (structuredSkillsSchema for type)
- recharts package installed

## Testing
- Component exports SkillsRadar
- Source contains RadarChart import from recharts
close_reason: Wave 3 complete: structured matching, candidate dedup, and radar chart implemented with passing tests

title: WS2: File storage setup (Vercel Blob)
description:
# WS2: File Storage Abstraction Layer

## Context
CV uploads need persistent file storage. The project uses Next.js on Vercel, so Vercel Blob Storage is the simplest option.

## What to Build

### New: src/lib/file-storage.ts
Abstract file storage behind a clean interface:
- uploadFile(buffer: Buffer, filename: string, contentType: string): Promise<{ url: string, pathname: string }>
- downloadFile(url: string): Promise<Buffer>
- deleteFile(url: string): Promise<void>

Implementation: Use @vercel/blob package.
- pnpm add @vercel/blob

Environment variable: BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN (add to .env.example)

### Why Abstraction
If we later switch to R2 or S3, only this file changes. All consumers use the abstract interface.

## Dependencies
- None (independent infrastructure task)

## Testing
- file-storage.ts exports uploadFile, downloadFile, deleteFile functions
- Source contains @vercel/blob import
close_reason: Vercel Blob file storage created: uploadFile, downloadFile, deleteFile in src/lib/file-storage.ts. mammoth installed for Word parsing.

title: Foundation: Shared Zod schemas (candidate-intelligence.ts + matching.ts)
description:
# Shared Zod Schemas — The Integration Contract

## Why This Is P0 / Foundation
All four workstreams depend on shared data shapes. WS2 (CV parser) outputs structuredSkillsSchema, WS3 (skills graph) reads it. WS1 (matcher) outputs matchAssessmentSchema, WS4 (reports) reads it. Without these schemas, no workstream can start.

## Files to Create

### src/schemas/candidate-intelligence.ts
Shared between WS2 and WS3:
- structuredSkillsSchema: { hard: [{name, proficiency (1-5), evidence}], soft: [{name, proficiency (1-5), evidence}] }
- parsedCVSchema: Full CV parse output (name, email, phone, role, location, skills, experience, education, certifications, languages, summary)
- Export all schemas and inferred types

### src/schemas/matching.ts
Shared between WS1 and WS4:
- tierSchema: z.enum(["knockout", "gunning", "process"])
- criterionResultSchema: { criterion, tier, passed (bool|null), stars (1-5|null), evidence, confidence }
- structuredMatchOutputSchema: { criteriaBreakdown, overallScore, knockoutsPassed, riskProfile, enrichmentSuggestions, recommendation, recommendationReasoning, recommendationConfidence }
- classifiedRequirementSchema: { criterion, tier, weight, source }

## Pattern to Follow
See existing src/schemas/job.ts for Zod schema patterns. Use .describe() on all fields for generateObject() compatibility. Export both schemas and inferred TypeScript types.

## Testing
Add validation tests in tests/schemas-validation.test.ts:
- Valid criterion result parses correctly
- Invalid stars (0, 6) rejected
- Missing evidence rejected
- All tier enum values accepted
- structuredSkillsSchema accepts valid hard/soft skills
close_reason: Foundation complete: shared Zod schemas and DB migration columns created

title: WS1: Structured match API route + server action
description:
# WS1: API Route & Server Action for Structured Matching

## Files to Create/Modify

### New: app/api/matches/structured/route.ts
POST endpoint that triggers structured evaluation for a job+candidate pair.
- Accepts { jobId: string, candidateId: string }
- Validates UUIDs with regex (pattern from app/matching/page.tsx)
- Calls extractRequirements(jobId) then runStructuredMatch(requirements, candidate)
- Stores results in jobMatches (update existing or create new)
- Returns structured match result
- Uses withApiHandler wrapper (src/lib/api-handler.ts)
- Publishes event via event-bus for SSE updates

### Modify: app/matching/actions.ts
Add runStructuredMatch(jobId, candidateId) server action:
- Calls the structured matching service
- Stores results in jobMatches JSONB columns
- Revalidates /matching path
- Returns { success: boolean, error?: string }
- Handles Gemini API errors gracefully (timeout, rate limit)

## Pattern to Follow
See existing app/api/matches/genereren/route.ts for the match generation API pattern. Use withRetry from src/lib/retry.ts for Gemini calls.

## Dependencies
- Structured matching service must exist
- Schema migration must be complete
close_reason: Wave 4 complete: match API, CV upload routes, skills profile section, report generator

title: WS4: Markdown.fast integration wrapper
description:
# WS4: Markdown.fast Integration

## New: src/lib/markdown-fast.ts

Wrapper around markdown.fast for publishing and sharing match reports.

### Functions
- publishReport(markdown: string, title: string): Promise<{ url: string, id: string }>
- getReport(id: string): Promise<string>
- revokeReport(id: string): Promise<void>

### Integration Approach
Investigate markdown.fast API/CLI:
- If it has a REST API: use fetch with auth token
- If it's CLI-only: use child_process.exec
- If it's a JS SDK: import directly

Environment variable: MARKDOWN_FAST_TOKEN (add to .env.example)

### Shareable URLs
Generated reports get a unique URL via markdown.fast. URLs are time-limited (30 days default, configurable).

### Fallback
If markdown.fast is unavailable, store markdown locally and serve from app/reports/[id]/page.tsx using streamdown for rendering.

## Dependencies
- None (independent infrastructure task)

## Testing
- Source exports publishReport, getReport, revokeReport
- Fallback logic exists for offline mode
close_reason: Wave 5 complete: match detail UI, CV upload sidebar, markdown.fast wrapper, skills graph tests — all tests passing

(6) Export now includes relatedContacts. (7) New API endpoint /api/gdpr/contacten/verwijder.

title: WS1: Structured matching service (per-criterion LLM evaluation)
description:
# WS1: Structured Matching Service — Mariënne-style Evaluation

## Context
Core of the structured matching pipeline. Takes classified requirements from the extraction service + candidate profile/CV and produces a full per-criterion evaluation with evidence.

## What to Build

### New file: src/services/structured-matching.ts

Single-call evaluation using Gemini 3.1 Pro. The key insight from Mariënne: evaluate EACH criterion individually against specific CV evidence.

Input:
- classifiedRequirements: ClassifiedRequirement[] (from requirement-extraction service)
- candidate: Candidate record OR raw CV text/PDF

Output: structuredMatchOutputSchema (from src/schemas/matching.ts)
- criteriaBreakdown: per-criterion results with evidence
- overallScore: 0-100 aggregate
- knockoutsPassed: boolean (all knockouts must pass)
- riskProfile: string[] of risk flags
- enrichmentSuggestions: string[] of suggestions
- recommendation: "go" | "no-go" | "conditional"
- recommendationConfidence: 0-100

### System Prompt (Dutch — Mariënne methodology)
```
Je bent een recruitment matching specialist. Evalueer de kandidaat tegen ELKE eis afzonderlijk.

Voor KNOCK-OUT criteria: Geef Ja/Nee met bewijs uit het CV.
Voor GUNNINGSCRITERIA: Geef 1-5 sterren met onderbouwing.
Voor PROCESEISEN: Noteer maar beoordeel niet.

Wees eerlijk en specifiek. Citeer concrete passages uit het CV als bewijs.
Als er geen bewijs is, zeg dan "Geen bewijs gevonden in CV".
```

### Architecture Decision
Uses google("gemini-3.1-pro") with generateObject(). The 1M token context window allows sending the ENTIRE CV + ALL requirements in a single call — no chunking needed. Native PDF support means we can send the raw PDF buffer directly.

### Integration with Existing Scoring
This SUPPLEMENTS the existing hybrid scoring (src/services/scoring.ts). The matchScore field is updated with the structured aggregate score. Existing rule-based scoring remains as fast pre-filter. Only run structured matching on candidates the recruiter wants to evaluate deeply.

## Dependencies
- Requirement extraction service (WS1 previous bead)
- Shared Zod schemas (foundation)
- Schema migration (for storing results)

## Testing
- Service exports runStructuredMatch function
- Source text contains Mariënne-style system prompt
- Source text contains generateObject call with structuredMatchOutputSchema
- Handles missing CV gracefully (returns error, not crash)
close_reason: Wave 3 complete: structured matching, candidate dedup, and radar chart implemented with passing tests

title: P2: Escape LIKE wildcards in search inputs
description: app/professionals/page.tsx:37 and app/api/opdrachten/zoeken/route.ts:23-31 pass raw user input into ilike patterns without escaping % and _ wildcards. A user searching for '%_%' would match everything. Add a utility to escape LIKE special chars before interpolation: function escapeLike(s: string) { return s.replace(/[%_\\]/g, '\\$&'); }
close_reason: Added escapeLike() utility to src/lib/helpers.ts. Applied to all 4 files with raw ilike patterns: professionals/page.tsx, api/opdrachten/zoeken/route.ts, services/jobs.ts, services/candidates.ts.

title: Phase 2: Scraper Operations — Config, History, Health
description:
## Background
Phase 2 builds the operational layer on top of Phase 1's scraper pipeline. While Phase 1 hard-codes platform configs, Phase 2 makes everything dynamic and observable through database-driven scraper configs, scrape history tracking, health monitoring, cache revalidation, and REST API endpoints.

## Why This Matters
Without this phase, there's no way to manage scrapers without code changes, no visibility into scrape history, and no way for the Phase 4 UI to display operational data.

## Technical Approach
- Add scraper_configs and scrape_results tables to Drizzle schema
- Create Motia event steps: RecordScrapeResult, RevalidateCache
- Create 5 API route steps (GET configs, PATCH config, POST trigger, GET history, GET health)
- Modify MasterScrape to load active configs from DB
- Seed initial Striive config row

## Success Criteria
- All 5 API endpoints return correct data
- PATCH toggle prevents scraping on next cron run
- Manual trigger via POST works end-to-end
- ScrapeResults recorded for every run
- Health endpoint shows warning when >3 failures in 24h
close_reason: Phase 2 complete: all 11 tasks done, 25 tests passing, schema pushed to Neon

Run drizzle-kit generate + push.

title: EPIC: Candidate Intelligence & Structured Matching
description:
# Candidate Intelligence & Structured Matching (Mariënne-style)

## Overview
Upgrade Motian's matching engine from a single numeric score to a 6-phase structured evaluation powered by Gemini 3.1 Pro. Four parallel workstreams:
- WS1: Structured matching pipeline (LLM-powered per-criterion evaluation)
- WS2: CV upload sidebar with AI parsing
- WS3: Skills graph visualization (radar + tags)
- WS4: Markdown.fast report integration

## Why
Current matching produces a meaningless single score (matchScore: 78). Recruiters need per-criterion evidence-based assessments suitable for government tenders. The Custom GPT Mariënne analysis shows the gold standard: tiered requirements (knockout/gunning/process), per-criterion star ratings with CV evidence, risk profiles, and go/no-go recommendations.

## Model
Gemini 3.1 Pro (released Feb 19 2026): 1M token context, native PDF processing, 65K token output, structured output via generateObject().

## Plan
docs/plans/2026-02-23-feat-candidate-intelligence-structured-matching-plan.md
close_reason: EPIC complete: all 22 beads closed across 6 waves. 4 workstreams delivered: WS1 structured matching pipeline, WS2 CV upload sidebar, WS3 skills graph visualization, WS4 report generation. 413 tests passing.

title: WS2: CV upload sidebar component (persistent Sheet)
description:
# WS2: Persistent CV Upload Sidebar

## Context
A right-side Sheet (drawer) component that persists across all routes. The recruiter can open it from any page, drag-and-drop a CV, and see the parsed profile.

## What to Build

### New: components/cv-upload-sidebar.tsx
Client component ("use client") using Sheet from components/ui/sheet.tsx.

Features:
1. **Trigger button** — Floating action button (bottom-right) or header icon with Upload + UserPlus icon
2. **Drag-and-drop zone** — Native HTML5 DnD API. Accepts .pdf and .docx. Shows visual feedback on dragover.
3. **File validation** — Max 20MB. Only PDF/Word. Show error for invalid types.
4. **Upload progress** — Loader2 spinner during upload + parsing
5. **Parsed profile preview** — Shows extracted name, role, skills (tags), experience count. Editable fields.
6. **Deduplication alert** — If duplicate found, show merge/create-new options
7. **Save button** — "Opslaan" creates/updates candidate. Shows success state.
8. **Mobile** — Sheet becomes bottom sheet on mobile (use Sheet side="bottom" on < md breakpoints)

### Modify: app/layout.tsx
Add <CvUploadSidebar /> at layout level so it persists across route changes. Use React state or context for open/close.

### UI Pattern
Follow existing opdrachten-sidebar.tsx patterns:
- ScrollArea for content overflow
- Search/filter patterns
- React Query for server state

### Dutch UI Strings
- "CV uploaden" — title
- "Sleep een CV hierheen of klik om te selecteren" — drop zone text
- "Opslaan" — save button
- "Kandidaat gevonden" — dedup match
- "Nieuw aanmaken" / "Samenvoegen" — dedup options

## Dependencies
- CV parsing service
- File storage
- Deduplication logic
- Upload API route

## Testing
- Component exports CvUploadSidebar
- Source contains Sheet import
- Source contains drag-and-drop handlers (onDragOver, onDrop)

(3) Migrate scraper page from shadcn semantic colors (text-muted-foreground, bg-card) to hardcoded hex system used everywhere else, OR migrate all pages to semantic colors (preferred long-term).

title: WS1: Requirement extraction service (Gemini 3.1 Pro)
description:
# WS1: Requirement Extraction & Tier Classification

## Context
Phase 1 of the structured matching pipeline. Before we can evaluate a candidate against job criteria, we need to extract and classify those criteria from the job posting.

## What to Build

### New file: src/services/requirement-extraction.ts

Uses Gemini 3.1 Pro via @ai-sdk/google generateObject() to extract requirements from a job record and classify each into a tier.

Input: Job record (title, description, requirements[], wishes[], competences[] from the existing jobs table)
Output: ClassifiedRequirement[] — each with { criterion, tier, weight, source }

### Tier Classification Rules (embedded in system prompt, Dutch):
- KNOCKOUT: Hard requirements from requirements[] with isKnockout: true, certifications, mandatory qualifications, years of experience thresholds. Binary pass/fail.
- GUNNING: Wishes from wishes[], soft skills, "nice to have" qualifications, domain experience. Scored 1-5 stars.
- PROCESS: Administrative requirements (availability date, security clearance, reference letters). Noted but not scored.

### System Prompt Design (Dutch)
Follow pattern in src/services/ai-enrichment.ts lines 51-78:
- Use google("gemini-3.1-pro") model
- generateObject() with classifiedRequirementSchema from src/schemas/matching.ts
- Rate limiting with withRetry from src/lib/retry.ts

### Caching
Consider caching extracted requirements per jobId (requirements don't change). Store in a new jobRequirements field or use in-memory cache.

## Dependencies
- Shared Zod schemas must exist (src/schemas/matching.ts)
- Schema migration must be complete (for any new columns)

## Testing (tests/structured-matching.test.ts)
- Service exports extractRequirements function
- Source text contains generateObject call
- Source text contains tier classification system prompt
- Zod schema validates output correctly
close_reason: Wave 2 complete: requirement extraction, CV parser, and skills tags all implemented with passing tests

title: WS4: Report generation service (markdown template)
description:
# WS4: Match Report Generation Service

## New: src/services/report-generator.ts

Takes structured match result + candidate profile + job details and generates a professional markdown report.

### Input
- match: jobMatches record with criteriaBreakdown, riskProfile, etc.
- candidate: candidates record (name, role, skills)
- job: jobs record (title, company, requirements)

### Output
Markdown string following a professional template.

### Report Template (Dutch)
```markdown
# Matchrapport: {candidate.name} — {job.title}

**Datum:** {date}
**Opdrachtgever:** {job.company}
**Beoordeling:** {recommendation} ({recommendationConfidence}%)

## Samenvatting
{recommendationReasoning}

## Knock-out Criteria
| Eis | Resultaat | Onderbouwing |
|-----|-----------|-------------|
{foreach knockout criterion}

## Gunningscriteria
| Criterium | Score | Onderbouwing |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
{foreach gunning criterion with stars}

## Risicoprofiel
{riskProfile items as bullet list}

## Aanbevelingen voor Verrijking
{enrichmentSuggestions as numbered list}
```

### GDPR Compliance
- Include: name, role, skills, match scores, evidence
- EXCLUDE: email, phone, address, personal notes
- Add GDPR notice at bottom of report

## Dependencies
- Structured matching (WS1) must produce results
- Shared schemas for match assessment data

## Testing
- Service exports generateReport function
- Report contains GDPR exclusion logic (no email/phone in output)
- Report follows markdown template structure
close_reason: Wave 4 complete: match API, CV upload routes, skills profile section, report generator

title: Foundation: Schema migration — add JSONB columns to jobMatches + candidates
description:
# Schema Migration — Additive JSONB Columns

## Why This Is P0 / Foundation
WS1 needs criteriaBreakdown, riskProfile, recommendation columns on jobMatches. WS2 needs skillsStructured, resumeRaw, education, certifications, languageSkills on candidates. All columns are nullable JSONB (additive-only, zero risk to existing data).

## File to Modify: src/db/schema.ts

### jobMatches table — add:
- criteriaBreakdown: jsonb("criteria_breakdown") — MatchCriterion[] from structuredMatchOutputSchema
- riskProfile: jsonb("risk_profile") — string[] of risk flags
- enrichmentSuggestions: jsonb("enrichment_suggestions") — string[] of suggestions
- recommendation: text("recommendation") — "go" | "no-go" | "conditional"
- recommendationConfidence: real("recommendation_confidence") — 0-100
- assessmentModel: text("assessment_model") — "marienne-v1" for version tracking

### candidates table — add:
- resumeRaw: text("resume_raw") — Full extracted CV text for re-processing
- resumeParsedAt: timestamp("resume_parsed_at") — When CV was last parsed
- skillsStructured: jsonb("skills_structured") — { hard: [...], soft: [...] } with proficiency
- education: jsonb("education") — Array of { degree, institution, year }
- certifications: jsonb("certifications") — string[]
- languageSkills: jsonb("language_skills") — Array of { language, level (CEFR) }

## Migration Steps
1. Edit src/db/schema.ts (add columns)
2. pnpm db:generate (create migration SQL)
3. pnpm db:push (apply to Neon database)

## Safety
All columns are nullable. No existing data is affected. No default values needed. Additive-only migration — zero downtime.

## Testing
Add schema structure tests in tests/schema-migration.test.ts:
- jobMatches table has criteriaBreakdown column
- jobMatches table has recommendation column
- candidates table has skillsStructured column
- candidates table has resumeRaw column
close_reason: Foundation complete: shared Zod schemas and DB migration columns created

title: 🚫 No Error Boundaries — zero error.tsx/loading.tsx
close_reason: Closed

title: ⚠️ Cron ignores per-platform schedule — config cronExpression decorative
close_reason: Closed

title: WS2: CV parsing service (Gemini 3.1 Pro native PDF)
description:
# WS2: CV Parsing Service — Gemini 3.1 Pro Native PDF

## Context
The keystone service that feeds all other workstreams. Parses a CV (PDF or Word) using Gemini 3.1 Pro's native PDF processing and outputs a structured candidate profile.

## What to Build

### New: src/services/cv-parser.ts

Uses google("gemini-3.1-pro") with generateObject() and file attachment.

Input: CV file buffer (PDF or extracted Word text) + MIME type
Output: parsedCVSchema (from src/schemas/candidate-intelligence.ts)

Key output fields:
- name, email, phone, role, location
- skills: { hard: [{name, proficiency 1-5, evidence}], soft: [{name, proficiency 1-5, evidence}] }
- experience: [{title, company, startYear, endYear, description}]
- education: [{degree, institution, year}]
- certifications: string[]
- languages: [{language, level (CEFR: A1-C2 or native)}]
- summary: 2-3 sentence professional summary

### System Prompt (Dutch)
```
Je bent een CV-parser voor een recruitment platform. Analyseer dit CV en extraheer alle relevante informatie.

Voor VAARDIGHEDEN: Schat het vaardigheidsniveau in (1=beginner, 5=expert) op basis van jaren ervaring, projecten, en certificeringen vermeld in het CV. Onderscheid harde vaardigheden (technisch, tools, talen) en zachte vaardigheden (communicatie, leiderschap, samenwerking).

Wees nauwkeurig. Als informatie ontbreekt, gebruik null. Gok niet.
```

### Gemini 3.1 Pro PDF Integration
The model can process PDF files natively (up to 900 pages). Send the raw PDF buffer as a file part in the message. No need for pdf-parse or other extraction libraries.

For Word (.docx): Extract text first using mammoth or similar, then send as text.

### Word Support
pnpm add mammoth (for .docx to text extraction)

## Dependencies
- Shared Zod schemas (parsedCVSchema)
- File storage (for persisting the raw file)

## Testing
- Service exports parseCV function
- Source contains gemini-3.1-pro model
- parsedCVSchema validates a sample output
- Handles empty/invalid PDF gracefully
close_reason: Wave 2 complete: requirement extraction, CV parser, and skills tags all implemented with passing tests

title: ESCO canonical skills matching rollout
description:
Implement an ESCO-first canonical skills architecture for candidates, jobs, and agent/tool inputs.

## Background
Current matching uses free-text skills plus rule-based/hybrid scoring. This fragments synonyms, weakens critical-skill reasoning, and prevents recruiter filters and agent surfaces from sharing a single taxonomy.

## Goal
Normalize inbound skill text to ESCO URIs, make ESCO scoring the primary skill signal with guardrails, and propagate canonical skills consistently across UI, API, chat, MCP, and voice.

## Required outcomes
- Canonical ESCO tables, imports, and link records exist.
- Existing candidate/job data can be backfilled without downtime.
- ESCO-first scoring can be enabled and rolled back through config.
- Critical low-confidence mappings go to review instead of silently degrading ranking.
- Canonical skills reach all recruiter and agent surfaces with parity.
- Unit and integration tests cover mapping, scoring, parity, and rollout safety.

## Source plan
See docs/plans/2026-03-05-feat-esco-skills-canonical-matching-plan.md.

title: Build ESCO schema, import, and backfill foundation
description:
Create the ESCO persistence layer and import/backfill foundation.

## Scope
- Add schema objects for `esco_skills`, `skill_aliases`, `candidate_skills`, `job_skills`, and mapping/review audit storage.
- Define uniqueness and indexing so reruns are idempotent.
- Import a pinned ESCO dataset version into the local database.
- Add rerunnable backfill/reconciliation jobs for existing candidate/job skill sources.
- Keep legacy free-text fields readable during rollout via dual-write/bridge behavior.

## Success criteria
- Re-running import/backfill does not duplicate rows.
- Existing candidates/jobs can be mapped without downtime.
- Dataset version and mapping provenance are auditable.

## Notes
Must account for current sources in `candidates.skills`, `candidates.skills_structured`, `jobs.requirements`, `jobs.wishes`, and `jobs.competences`.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in this session

title: Implement shared ESCO mapper and critical review queue
description:
Build the shared ESCO mapping service and critical-review flow.

## Scope
- Implement normalization and lookup service in `src/services/esco.ts`.
- Support deterministic normalization, exact/alias match, semantic fallback, and explicit confidence scoring.
- Persist strategy, evidence, thresholds, and review-required decisions.
- Create critical-skill review queue behavior for low-confidence critical mappings only.
- Wire mapping into candidate/job ingestion and enrichment entry points.

## Success criteria
- Candidate/job/tool skill inputs resolve to canonical ESCO URIs with confidence/evidence.
- Critical low-confidence mappings generate review items instead of silent drops.
- Mapping behavior is configurable through explicit threshold settings.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in this session

title: Cut over scoring to ESCO-first with guardrails and telemetry
description:
Replace legacy skill scoring with ESCO-first scoring plus telemetry and rollback safety.

## Scope
- Add `src/services/esco-scoring.ts`.
- Route `computeMatchScore` skill scoring through canonical ESCO logic.
- Weight critical/required skills explicitly and trigger fallback when critical confidence is too low.
- Propagate ESCO reasoning into auto-match and match records.
- Record side-by-side telemetry during rollout so `hybrid-v1` and `esco-hybrid-v1` can be compared.
- Ensure rollback to legacy scoring is a config/flag change, not a code revert.

## Success criteria
- Production scoring path can run ESCO-first with guardrails.
- Fallback events are visible in reasoning and metrics.
- Rollback path is documented and operationally safe.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in this session

title: Ship canonical skill parity across UI, API, chat, MCP, and voice
description:
Deliver canonical-skill parity across recruiter UI, APIs, and all agent surfaces.

## Scope
- Update recruiter APIs and UI filters/suggesters to use canonical skills.
- Expose canonical skill payloads consistently in candidate/job/match endpoints.
- Update chat tools, MCP tools, and voice-agent flows to use the shared canonical service contract.
- Preserve cross-surface parity learned from prior agent/UI and voice parity incidents.

## Success criteria
- UI, API, chat, MCP, and voice return coherent canonical skill data.
- Recruiters can search/filter on ESCO labels instead of loose free text.
- No surface drifts onto stale or incompatible contracts.
close_reason: Implemented and verified in this session

title: Add ESCO rollout tests, metrics, and runbook
description:
Finish verification, observability, and rollout documentation for the ESCO rollout.

## Scope
- Add unit tests for mapping, thresholding, idempotency, and scoring guardrails.
- Add integration/parity tests across UI/API/chat/MCP/voice and backfill/rescore flows.
- Track confidence distribution, fallback rate, review backlog, and Precision@3 baseline/post-cutover snapshots.
- Write operator runbook for dataset import, backfill order, feature-flag cutover, and rollback.

## Success criteria
- Test suite covers the real callback/service chain, not only mocked units.
- Metrics support 4-6 week precision evaluation against a stable pre-cutover baseline.
- Release can be executed and rolled back from documented operational steps.
close_reason: Implemented locally with rollout metrics artifacts, regression comparison tooling, tests, and rollback runbook. Remaining epic work is staging/runtime validation, not missing code.
